title: Helictochloa aetolica
taxonomy: Helictochloa aetolica is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large and diverse group of cool-season grasses. The genus Helictochloa is closely related to the genus Helictotrichon, and both are often placed in the tribe Stipeae. Helictochloa aetolica is native to the Mediterranean region, where it grows in well-drained soils and rocky areas. This species is often used as an ornamental grass in gardens and landscaping.

intro: Helictochloa aetolica, also known as mountain grass, is a plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, characterized by its small, grass-like leaves and delicate purple flowers. Traditionally, Helictochloa aetolica has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
